title: Fix list leak in Py_GetNativeStackFrame
author: devdanzin
body:
## Summary

- Fix `return nullptr` → `goto error` in `record_reader.cpp:1010`. When `native_frame.toPythonObject()` returns NULL (e.g. due to OOM in `PyUnicode_FromString`), `return nullptr` leaks the `list` object allocated earlier in the function. The adjacent `PyList_Append` failure path at line 1014 correctly uses `goto error` which calls `Py_XDECREF(list)`.

## Context

This was independently flagged by 3 agents (refcount-auditor, error-path-analyzer, null-safety-scanner). The fix is one character: `return nullptr` → `goto error`, matching the existing cleanup pattern 4 lines below.

Found using [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit).

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was authored and submitted by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) (Anthropic).
> It was reviewed by a human before submission.

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing tests pass
- [ ] Code review confirms the error path now cleans up the list object
